# pastehtml.dev — full reference

> pastehtml.dev publishes a single self-contained HTML (or Markdown) file and
> returns a private, shareable link. Each paste is served on its own isolated
> origin (`<token>.pastehtml.dev`) where its scripts and localStorage work but
> cannot touch any other paste or the app itself. Anonymous publishing needs no
> account; an account API key (prefix `pht_`) lets an agent publish into a
> user's dashboard and organize pastes into folders. This is the complete
> machine reference. The short integration guide is at /llms.txt.

## Concepts

- Paste: one stored HTML document. It has a random 32-char lowercase `token`,
  an optional user-chosen `custom_subdomain`, an optional password, an optional
  folder, and a `views_count`. Pastes are immutable in identity: the token never
  changes and a paste can never be deleted by anyone. Content is changed only by
  "republishing" (an update), which keeps the same token and share link.
- Origins & isolation: the share link `live_url` is `https://<subdomain>.pastehtml.dev/`,
  a dedicated origin per paste. Untrusted document scripts run there with real
  localStorage, fully isolated from other pastes and from pastehtml.dev. The app's
  own pages and API live only on `pastehtml.dev` (and app hosts), never on paste
  origins, so a paste cannot reach the API or frame the app under its origin.
- Update token: anonymous creation reveals a one-time `update_token` (only its
  digest is stored). It authorizes updates while the paste stays anonymous. A
  normal update does NOT rotate it. Claiming the paste into an account retires it;
  afterward the account key is the sole update credential.
- Account API key: created by a signed-in user at /api_keys, shown once, stored
  only as an HMAC digest, prefixed `pht_`. May be unscoped or scoped to one
  default folder. Treat like a password: never put it in published HTML, paste
  content, URLs, logs, or a user-visible answer.

## POST /api/pastes — publish

Auth: none (anonymous) OR an account API key. Body forms (equivalent):

    # multipart file
    curl -F "file=@plan.html" https://pastehtml.dev/api/pastes
    # raw body (filename optional; defaults to untitled.html, or untitled.md for text/markdown)
    curl --data-binary @plan.html -H "Content-Type: text/html" \
      "https://pastehtml.dev/api/pastes?filename=plan.html"

Account key (any header form):

    Authorization: Bearer pht_...
    X-PasteHTML-API-Key: pht_...
    X-API-Key: pht_...

Options (form fields or query params):
- `password=<secret>` — gate the paste behind a password. Allowed anonymously.
- `custom_subdomain=<slug>` — claim `<slug>.pastehtml.dev`. REQUIRES an account
  key (401 otherwise); prevents anonymous squatting. Slug: lowercase letters,
  digits, hyphens; 1–63 chars; cannot start/end with a hyphen; not reserved.
- `folder_id=<int>` or `folder_name=<str>` — file into a folder. REQUIRES an
  account key (401 otherwise). `folder_name` finds-or-creates. Pass at most one.
- Scoped keys always publish into their folder and reject a different folder_id/
  folder_name (422) or clear_folder (422).

Response 201 (JSON): `token`, `title` (from `<title>`), `custom_subdomain`,
`password_protected`, `views_count`, `folder` (null or `{id,name}`), `owner`
(null or `{id}`), `account_paste` (bool), `live_url`, `url`, `raw_url`,
`render_url`, `markdown_url`, and `update_token` (anonymous only — omitted for
account-key publishes).

Errors: 422 `{errors:[...]}` invalid content/filename/options; 401
`{error}` an invalid/revoked key, or a folder/custom_subdomain requested without
a key. An invalid `pht_` bearer is never silently downgraded to anonymous.

## PATCH /api/pastes/:token — update (republish)

`:token` is the paste's token. Authorize with EITHER the update token OR an
account key that owns the paste:

    # by update token
    curl -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Bearer <update_token>" \
      --data-binary @plan.html -H "Content-Type: text/html" \
      https://pastehtml.dev/api/pastes/<token>

    # by owning account key
    curl -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Bearer pht_..." -F "file=@plan.html" \
      https://pastehtml.dev/api/pastes/<token>

Claim an anonymous paste into an account: send the account key in `Authorization`
and the paste secret in `X-Update-Token: <update_token>`. After claiming, the
update token stops working. Accepts new content (same two body forms) and/or
options (`custom_subdomain`, `password`, `clear_password=1`, `folder_id`,
`folder_name`, `clear_folder=1`). Content is optional if options are supplied.

Response 200: same fields as create, minus `update_token`. Errors: 403 wrong/
missing update token or a key that doesn't own the paste; 404 unknown token;
422 invalid content/options.

## Folders — GET/POST /api/folders

Account key required.

    curl -H "Authorization: Bearer pht_..." https://pastehtml.dev/api/folders
    curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer pht_..." -d "name=Roadmap" https://pastehtml.dev/api/folders
    curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer pht_..." -d "folder[name]=Roadmap" https://pastehtml.dev/api/folders

GET lists `{folders:[{id,name,pastes_count}]}`. Unscoped keys list every folder;
scoped keys list only their scoped folder. POST creates a folder (unscoped keys
only; scoped keys get 403). 401 without a valid key.

## Read a paste back

- `GET /p/<token>/raw` — exact bytes, `text/plain`, never rewritten in transit.
  Prefer this for byte-exact reads (diff before update). ETag-revalidated.
- `GET /p/<token>/render` — same bytes as `text/html` inside a CSP sandbox.
- `GET /p/<token>/markdown` — a lossy best-effort Markdown view (`text/markdown`).
- `GET /p/<token>` — the inspector page (copy link, preview, highlighted source).
- `https://<subdomain>.pastehtml.dev/` — the live document on its own origin.
- Pastes with a `custom_subdomain` also answer at `/p/<custom_subdomain>` and its
  `/raw|/render|/markdown` variants, in addition to `/p/<token>`.
- Password-protected pastes require the password gate before raw/render/markdown/live.
  All paste content is served with `X-Robots-Tag: noindex`.

## Publish Markdown

A `.md`/`.markdown` filename or a `text/markdown` content type is rendered
server-side into a styled, self-contained HTML page (GitHub-flavored: tables,
task lists, strikethrough, footnotes, autolinks; fenced `mermaid` blocks become
diagrams). The stored paste is HTML, so the response shape is identical to an
HTML publish. Raw HTML embedded in the Markdown is dropped — upload plain HTML
for arbitrary markup.

## MCP server (OAuth, no API key)

A coding agent that speaks the Model Context Protocol can skip the HTTP API
above and connect to the remote MCP server at `https://pastehtml.dev/mcp`
(Streamable HTTP). It authorizes over OAuth in the user's browser — there is no
`pht_` key to handle.

    claude mcp add --transport http pastehtml https://pastehtml.dev/mcp   # Claude Code
    codex mcp add pastehtml --url https://pastehtml.dev/mcp               # Codex

The first connection opens a browser consent screen; once approved, the agent
acts inside that account. Auth is OAuth 2.1 + PKCE with Dynamic Client
Registration (no client secret); scopes are `mcp:read`, `mcp:pastes:write`, and `mcp:folders:write`; the user
inspects and revokes agents under "Connected agents". Ten tools are exposed —
pastes are permanent, so there is deliberately no delete-paste tool:

- `create_paste` — publish a new HTML or Markdown paste (optionally into a folder)
- `update_paste` — republish an existing paste's content (overwrites it)
- `configure_paste` — change a paste's password, custom subdomain, or folder
- `get_paste` — fetch one paste's metadata, URLs, and stored content
- `get_paste_stats` — aggregate view analytics for a paste
- `list_pastes` — page through the account's pastes (optionally by folder)
- `list_folders` — list folders with their paste counts
- `create_folder` — create a new, empty folder
- `rename_folder` — rename a folder
- `delete_folder` — delete a folder (its pastes survive, unfiled)

Everything in this reference — the 2 MB content limit, self-contained
documents, always surfacing the `live_url` — applies equally over MCP, with one
encoding caveat: a whole MCP request is capped at 8 MB, and paste content travels
as a JSON string. Escaping expands it (quotes/backslashes double; some encoders
emit six-byte `\uXXXX` escapes for `<`, `>`, `&`). Typical clients leave `<`, `>`,
`&` raw and publish the full 2 MB comfortably; a client whose encoder escapes them
should keep such character-heavy content below ~1.3 MB to stay under the cap.
Operators can disable open Dynamic Client Registration with the
`MCP_DYNAMIC_REGISTRATION_DISABLED` environment variable.

## Limits & rules

- File: `.html`, `.htm`, `.md`, `.markdown`; UTF-8; at most 2 MB.
- Give the document a meaningful `<title>` — it becomes the display title.
- Make it self-contained: inline CSS/JS or load from CDNs.
- Rate limits (per IP): publish/update 20/minute and 1,000/day; folder
  discovery/creation 60/minute and 1,000/day.
- Password max 72 bytes (bcrypt limit). Never publish secrets unless the document
  itself encrypts them client-side.

## Worked guides (live, self-contained pages)

Read each guide to reproduce its technique, then publish the result through the
API — or just share the guide link with the user.

- Password-protected / client-side-encrypted page:
  https://pastehtml.dev/lock-it-up
- Markdown → styled HTML page (no-server, in-browser build with live preview):
  https://pastehtml.dev/mark-it-down
- How this site was built by a human + an AI in a day:
  https://pastehtml.dev/making-of

## Agent etiquette

1. After publishing/updating, always show the user the `live_url`; mention `url`
   for the source view.
2. Use a supplied account key only for the request; never reveal it back.
3. Save any `update_token` durably (e.g. `.pastehtml/<token>.update-token`) so a
   later "update that doc" works. Account-key publishes may omit it — use the key.
4. To revise a published document, PATCH the existing paste; don't publish a
   duplicate, so the user's shared link stays current.
